Change this function to improve its performance. Specifically:
- Reduce the time complexity below O(n²).
- Keep the public signature identical.
- Write a 3-line docstring explaining the new approach.
- Preserve all existing behavior; add 2 unit tests proving equivalence.

Here is an example of the style I want:

<example>
def dedupe(items: list[str]) -> list[str]:
    """Return items with duplicates removed, preserving first-seen order.

    O(n) via an auxiliary set. See benchmarks/dedupe.py for timing.
    """
    seen: set[str] = set()
    return [x for x in items if not (x in seen or seen.add(x))]
</example>

If the function has side effects I haven't noticed, flag them and ask
before rewriting. Otherwise apply the change and show only the diff.
